name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.authorization.CredentialMetadata
comment: Credential metadata used for caching and serialization of provider authentication data

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.authorization.AuthCredential
comment: Authentication credential model (token/api key) stored in cache and serialized/deserialized at runtime

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.payin.PayInTransaction
comment: Domain entity representing a PayIn transaction, used in persistence and serialization processes

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.payin.PayInTransactionResult
comment: Generic PayIn transaction result containing dynamic providerRequest/providerResponse payloads, requires reflection for JSON serialization in GraalVM

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.payout.PayOutTransaction
comment: Domain entity representing a PayOut transaction, used in persistence and integration flows

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.payout.PayOutTransactionResult
comment: Generic PayOut transaction result with dynamic payloads, requires reflection for proper serialization in GraalVM

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.provider.ProviderIntegrationLog
comment: Persistence entity used to store provider request/response payloads in JSON format for auditing and traceability

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.provider.ProviderTransaction
comment: Normalized provider transaction entity used for internal processing and storage

name: com.tumipay.microservice.domain.model.transaction.AdapterTransactionResult
comment: Standard adapter transaction result that encapsulates provider response data and is used in mapping and serialization

name: java.time.Instant
comment: Java time type used across domain models, requires reflection support for serialization in GraalVM

comment: Java time type used for latency and TTL calculations, requires reflection support in GraalVM

name: com.tumipay.microservice.infrastructure.adapter.output.http.standard.response.GatewayWebhookResponse
comment: Gateway webhook response DTO used by WebClient (Jackson) for deserialization in GraalVM

comment: Nested DTO inside GatewayWebhookResponse for successful responses, requires reflection for Jackson mapping

name: com.tumipay.microservice.infrastructure.adapter.output.http.standard.request.GatewayWebhookRequest
comment: Outbound webhook request DTO serialized to JSON using Jackson inside WebClient
